title: PROMPT block content
description: Carries the system prompt + user-prompt template + optional model config that the SLM / LLM workers execute. The `kind` discriminator groups prompts by purpose so the engine and admin UI can pick the right block at the right pipeline stage. Per architecture §3 — block library is the source of truth.

kind.description: What stage of the pipeline this prompt drives. CLASSIFICATION runs at the cascade tier; SCAN runs at stage ④ (PII / PHI / PCI / CUSTOM) keyed by `scanType`; METADATA_EXTRACTION runs after scans, keyed by `metadataSchemaId`; GENERAL is operator-defined ad-hoc usage.

systemPrompt.description: System message sent verbatim to the model. At least one of `systemPrompt` / `userPromptTemplate` must be present.

userPromptTemplate.description: Mustache-style template for the user message. Supported placeholders include `{{documentText}}`, `{{categoryId}}`, `{{categoryName}}`, `{{sensitivity}}` — workers substitute before dispatch. At least one of `systemPrompt` / `userPromptTemplate` must be present.

scanType.description: Required when `kind == SCAN`. Selects the scan family. PII / PHI / PCI gate downstream redaction policies; CUSTOM is operator-defined.

metadataSchemaId.description: Required when `kind == METADATA_EXTRACTION`. References a `MetadataSchema` document; the prompt extracts that schema's fields from the document text.

applicableCategoryIds.description: Categories this prompt applies to. Empty = global (matches all categories). Non-empty = scoped to those category ids — same convention as the hub-component entities (CSV #31–34).

model.description: Optional per-prompt model override. When absent, the worker uses its configured tier default (Anthropic Haiku for SLM, Anthropic Sonnet for LLM, the configured Ollama model for self-hosted).

provider.description: Backend that should run this prompt. Workers reject mismatched providers — an SLM worker won't run a prompt with `provider: OLLAMA` if the worker isn't configured for Ollama.

temperature.description: Sampling temperature. 0 = deterministic; classification / scan prompts usually want low values.

maxTokens.description: Hard cap on output tokens. Workers may reduce further to satisfy the per-replica budget.

outputFormat.description: Expected response shape. JSON is the default for CLASSIFICATION / SCAN / METADATA_EXTRACTION — the worker enforces JSON-mode output where the provider supports it. TEXT is plain-text passthrough for GENERAL prompts.
